rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION $586.00 U.S. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioner'S First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Amy Shearer; Declaration Of Tracy Sacca (15-006)
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 21, 2018, LINE 1 PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioner'S First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed; motion granted. Responses to be served by July 16, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.= DM

rulings.Case Title: WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI VS. AMIRA NATURE FOODS LTD.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 21, 2018, Line 3, PETITIONER WILSON SONSINI GOODRICH & ROSATI'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Plaintiff Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati's petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. No opposition filed. Based on the arbitration award and petition, WSGR is entitled to judgment in its favor and against defendant Amira Nature Foods Ltd. in the amount of $1,449,630.85, comprised of the principal amount of $1,132,622.54 plus prejudgment interest thereon from November 5, 2015 through June 21, 2018 at the rate of ten percent per year of $297,584.94 plus arbitrator compensation and expenses of $19,423.37 plus costs per the CCP and CRC. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order and proposed judgment submitted by WSGR. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL T JENKS VS. 346 FIRST STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, A NON PROFIT
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 21, 2018, LINE 4 PLAINTIFF MICHAEL JENKS MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court's preliminary view before argument is: Based on the pleadings to date, it appears that Plaintiff's medical records are at issue given the allegations in the discovery responses. In determining relevance, the Court looks not only to the operative complaint but other pleadings. Here, Plaintiff's discovery responses intertwine his medical conditions with the allegations against Defendant. These allegations include exemplary damages based upon Defendants abuse of Plaintiff's medical condition. Moreover, based on communications between counsel, it appears that a Second Amended Complaint will be filed shortly encompassing these allegations. If medical records are ordered produced, the documents would be first produced to Plaintiff's counsel for a review. Any documents that Plaintiff's counsel asserts should be withheld on privacy or privilege would then be submitted to the Court for an in-camera review. The Court notes that Defendants have withdrawn their subpoena to DLA for Plaintiff's employment records. = DM

rulings.Case Title: KELLY KIMBALL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 21, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFF KELLY KIMBALL'S Motion For Leave To File A Third Amended Complaint. Plaintiff Kelly Kimball's motion for leave to file a third amended complaint is denied as to Mr. Kimball's proposed eighth cause of action for breach of contract and granted as to Mr. Kimball's proposed ninth cause of action for violation of the Unfair Competition Law, Business and Professions Code 17200 et seq. The proposed breach of contract claim is a damages claim premised on an alleged violation of the owner move-in provisions of the San Francisco Rent Ordinance and thus is time-barred per CCP 340(1) as reflected in the court's ruling on the most recent motion for judgment on the pleadings filed by defendants Anna Kihagi and Xelan Prop 1, LLC. (Menefee v. Ostwari (1991) 228 Cal. App. 3d 239, 245). The allegations in the proposed breach of contract claim regarding the CCSF v. Kihagi case do not avoid the statute of limitations of section 340(1) or provide for a new or different basis for Mr. Kimball to seek damages for the alleged illegal owner move-in by defendants. In contrast to the proposed breach of contract claim, the proposed UCL claim is not a damages claim and thus is not governed by CCP 340(1) and instead is governed by the four year limitations periods applicable to all UCL claims. Menefee is not applicable to the UCL claim because, by its express terms, that decision only applies to damages claims. A prevailing plaintiff on a UCL claim "may not receive damages, much less treble damages "and is "generally limited to injunctive relief and restitution." (Cel-Tech Communications, Inc. (1999) 20 Cal. 4th 163, 179). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Kimball is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAUL VASQUEZ VS. NATIONAL DEFAULT SERVICING CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Quash Subpoena And Motion For Protective Order Re: Re: May 16 Notice Of Deposition Subpoena For Personal Appearance And Production Of Documents And Thing Of Plaintiffs Attorney Marc Voisenat
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 21, 2018, LINE 6 PLAINTIFF RAUL VASQUEZ'S Motion Quash Subpoena And Motion For Protective Order Re: Re: May 16 Notice Of Deposition Subpoena For Personal Appearance And Production Of Documents And Thing Of Plaintiffs Attorney Marc Voisenat Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. California case law is clear that the deposition of counsel is to be allowed only in extraordinary situations. The fact that counsel may be a percipient witness does not mean that the deposition will be allowed unless there are no alternative means of obtaining the information. Here, the Court finds that Defendant has not shown that there no alternative means of obtaining the information including by taking the deposition of Plaintiff. This ruling is without prejudice to Defendant filing a motion for reconsideration if its efforts to obtain the information are unsuccessful. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = DM

rulings.Case Title: SANDRA DEES VS. BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 21, 2018, LINE 8 PLAINTIFF SANDRA DEES' Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Plaintiff is entitled to take the PMK deposition of BART regardless of the testimony of the percipient witnesses. The Court understands Defendant's position that it has no information beyond that adduced in the depositions of the train operator and station agent, but Plaintiff may still solicit the agency's official corporate position by a PMK deposition. Any investigation will be protected by the attorney-client/work product privilege if it was directed by an attorney. Any documents withheld on privilege or other grounds shall be placed on a privilege log. The deposition (and production of documents) shall occur at a mutually agreed upon date before the discovery cutoff unless otherwise agreed by counsel. Plaintiff's counsel is admonished to not assert unnecessary colloquy and statements during depositions as it does nothing to advance the parties search for the truth through discovery in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = DM

rulings.Case Title: III-INTERACTIVE LLC DBA DIVISION D VS. ZEPHYER DIGITAL INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Verification Of Discovery Response, Production Of Documents, Supplemental Responses To Interrogatories And Requests For Admissions
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, JUNE 21, 2018, LINE 9 PLAINTIFF III-INTERACTIVE LLC'S Motion To Compel Verification Of Discovery Response, Production Of Documents, Supplemental Responses To Interrogatories And Requests For Admissions Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The issues raised in the motion have been resolved with the exception of Defendant's response to Form Interrogatory No. 17.1 and the agreed upon production of documents re RFPD Nos. 4, 6-12, 17, 20 and 21. Defendant shall serve a supplemental response to 17.1 and indicate whether there are any responsive documents to the RFPDs at issue by July 10, 2018. Sanctions denied as the Court believes that additional meet and confer discussions could have obviated the need for this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = DM

rulings.Case Title: LAURENCE VINOCUR VS. STREAMLIGHT, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And (Proposed) Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 21, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF LAURENCE VINOCUR'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And (Proposed) Consent Judgment. Plaintiff Laurence Vinocur's motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement with defendant Streamlight, Inc. is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order and proposed judgment submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO ALAMEDA COUNTY ** BEACON RESIDENTIAL COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION VS. ANN RANKIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ann Rankin Individually And Dba The Law Offices Of Ann Rankin'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 21, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT ANN RANKIN Individually And Dba The Law Offices Of Ann Rankin'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue. Defendant Ann Rankin's motion to change venue is granted. The case is transferred to Alameda Superior Court. Plaintiff Beacon Residential Community Association does not oppose the motion. Plaintiff is required to pay all costs of transfer because the motion was based on the grounds that San Francisco is not a proper venue for this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Rankin is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: REVPAR COLLECTIVE INC. DBA STASH HOTEL REWARDS VS. SYNCHRONY FINANCIAL ET AL

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 21, 2018, Line 8, DEFENDANT SYNCHRONY FINANCIAL, SYNCHRONY BANK Motion To Seal. Defendants Synchrony Financial and Synchrony Bank's motion to seal the unredacted version of the complaint is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)
